items.title: 💳 An important lesson for all credit card users to learn ! ⚠️ Unlike UPI or Net Banking, where money is transferred to the beneficiary almost instantly, credit card transactions are not settled immediately. In most cases, a credit card transaction takes 1–2 days to settle. This characteristic has both advantages and disadvantages. ✅ The Advantage: Since the transaction takes time to settle and reach the beneficiary’s account, if you notice any suspicious or fraudulent transaction, you can immediately report it to the card issuer. In many cases, the transaction can be put on hold or even reversed before settlement. This is not possible with most UPI transactions as the money is transferred instantly in UPI. ⚠️ The Disadvantage: If you’re trying to meet the spend deadline for a credit card offer, milestone benefit, welcome bonus, or any other benefit calculated on a calendar month basis, many card issuers consider the settlement date to determine eligibility, not the actual transaction date. And because if this, you may miss the deadline. 📅 So, wherever possible, don’t wait until the deadline to complete your spending. Make the transaction a couple of days in advance to avoid missing out on valuable benefits. ❓Have you ever missed a credit card offer or milestone because you transacted on the last day, but the issuer considered the settlement date and denied the benefit? 💬 Share your experience in the comments! 👇 ⸻ 📘 Visit SpendWisely Blog: Detailed credit card reviews & guides 👉 https://spendwisely.in Some links may be referral links in the above post. items.title: 🚢 ICICI EPM and its iShop portal appear to be a sinking ship… 📉 With the steep hike in the eligibility criteria, onboarding of new customers has become negligible. 🚫💳 👉For existing customers like me, EPM (just like Infinia) was primarily a gift card buying card. 🎁 With the discontinuation of Amazon Pay and Swiggy Gift Cards, I’ve had very little use for this card over the last two months. Now, with the further reduction in the number of available gift card brands, its usage is going to be even lower. 📉😕 That said, it still offers some very useful gift cards like Flipkart, Amazon Shopping, Amazon Fresh, BigBasket, Myntra, Nykaa, Ajio, NCD, Westside, Blinkit, Domino’s, MMT, etc. ✅✅✅ 👉 With HSBC Premier already in my wallet for flight ✈️ and hotel 🏨 bookings (and, of course, almost all non-gift card online & offline spends 🛍️), the use of my ICICI EPM card is now likely to be limited mainly to gift cards. 🤷‍♂️ 🤔 Let’s see how things turn out before my EPM renewal in March 2027. 🗓️ Around the same time, the future of my Infinia should also become clear. It’ll be interesting to see which of these two premium cards survives in my credit card portfolio… or maybe neither, as I’m not chasing Infinia’s ₹18 lakh annual spend requirement just to retain the card. 💳 If that happens, HSBC Premier (along with Kiwi for UPI 📲) will give me the simplest credit card portfolio I’ve had in many years. ✨😊 👉One thing that still goes in favour of EPM is the cost of keeping the card. Against the ₹14,759 annual fee, you get back 12,500 reward points. 💰 👉Another unique use case for me is the Priority Pass add-on card for my 12-year-old son. As far as I know, no other card currently offers a Priority Pass to a minor. Although, HSBC Premier’s guest access can bridge this gap if EPM eventually disappears from my card portfolio. But as we all know, even 8–9 months is a long enough time for everything to change in the ever-changing world of credit cards. 🔄💳 What are your views on ICICI EPM after the recent changes? Share your thoughts in the comments. 👇💬 #creditcard #spendwisely #creditcard
